DISCOVERY 612, NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS- DEFENDANT KAUFMAN DOLOWICH & VOLUCK LLP'S MOTION TO COMPEL AMENDED RESPONSES AND COMPLETE DOCUMENTS IN RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, IS MOOT AS RESPONSES HAVE BEEN SERVED. THE COURT FINDS THE OBJECTIONS AND THE LATE RESPONSES WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND CROSS COMPLAINANTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $780, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And For Sanctions;
Off Calendar Sep-08-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Daniel W. Hager, Atty. for defendant and moving party: Michael A. Vazquez, Michael Estrada, Vazquez, Estrada & dumont, LLP.
Robert E. White, Atty. for Plaintiff and Opposing party: Kaufman, Dolowich & Voluck, LLP

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendants Michael Vaszquez, Michael Estrada, Vasquez, Estrada & Dumont, LLP's Motion For Protective Order is granted as the depositions of the Person Most Knowledgeable and the individual depositions shall proceed simultaneously. Defendants shall present their person most knowledgeable, stating the topics enumerated in the notice on which the witness is prepared to testify, and that person also shall be deposed in his individual capacity. Subsequent individuals shall be deposed on those topics for which he is presented as the person most knowledgeable and in his personal capacity. The dates for the depositions shall be agreed upon by counsel after meet and confer and shall be completed no later than September 30, 2009. Sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.
